title: Grab MSI s RTX 5080 gaming laptop for just over $2,000 offers fast 240 Hz QHD+ display, dual storage slots, and expandable DDR5 memory
summary: This MSI gaming laptop offers high-end performance at a competitive price with its 240Hz QHD+ display, Core Ultra 9 CPU, and RTX 5080 GPU.
insight: Competitive high-end gaming laptop at a relatively affordable price point.

title: Lenovo hikes Legion Go 2 handheld gaming PC to almost $3,000 for 2 TB model Handheld now costs more than AMD's Strix Halo devices despite relatively weaker Z2 Extreme chip
summary: Lenovo has significantly raised the prices of Legion Go 2 handhelds, with the 2 TB model increasing from $1,480 to $2,850, a 93% hike.
insight: Lenovo's pricing may deter handheld gaming PC adoption due to affordability concerns.

title: Iran's forced nationwide internet blackout becomes second-longest on record as it passes 1,000 hours offline possessing Starlink terminals punishable by death, country using 'military-grade jamming' against service
summary: Iran's nationwide Internet blackout has surpassed 1,000 hours, becoming second-longest on record. The shutdown is enforced with threats of death for possessing Starlink terminals. Military-grade jamming is being used to block internet services.
insight: Iran's drastic measures severely impact its citizens' access to information and freedom.

title: Tiny 3-inch cube PCs bring a splash of color to the passive PC market with red, orange, green and blue options Intel Twin Lake-powered Kubb Mini PCs start at $500
summary: Kubb's compact 3-inch cube PCs offer colorful options starting at $500, with 16GB RAM and 256GB SSDs.
insight: Mini PCs bring affordable, high-performance computing to home users.

title: Linux lays down the law on AI-generated code, says yes to Copilot, no to AI slop, and humans take the fall for mistakes after months of fierce debate, Torvalds and maintainers come to an agreement
summary: Linus Torvalds and Linux kernel maintainers have reached an agreement on AI-generated code, accepting Copilot while rejecting sloppy AI outputs. Humans will be held accountable for mistakes after months of debate. This decision will shape the future of AI-assisted development in Linux.
insight: Human accountability ensures AI-assisted code quality and reliability in Linux.

title: Veteran Microsoft engineer says original Task Manager was only 80KB so it could run smoothly on 90s computers original utility used a smart technique to determine whether it was the only running instance
summary: The original Windows Task Manager was optimized to run smoothly on 90s computers at just 80KB. Veteran engineer Dave Plummer implemented several clever techniques to minimize performance impact. This allowed the utility to run while being the only instance.
insight: Optimizing for minimal footprint maximizes system resource efficiency.

title: Tech enthusiast gets Doom to run on a 40-year-old printer controller ancient Agfa Compugraphic 9000PS came with a Motorola 68020 onboard for fast processing
summary: A tech enthusiast successfully ran Doom on a 40-year-old Agfa Compugraphic 9000PS printer controller, showcasing the system's potential and capabilities despite its age.
insight: Retro hardware can still deliver surprising performance and nostalgic gaming experiences.

title: Keychron Q6 Ultra 8K Review: 660 hours of battery life at 8 KHz
summary: The Keychron Q6 Ultra 8K features 8 KHz polling and 660 hours battery life, but its keycaps and switches have drawbacks.
insight: Impressive battery life meets gaming demands with high polling rate.

title: Startup secures $30 million contract to 3D print jet engines for the USAF company to test and develop small turbojets for drones and long-range weapons
summary: Beehive Industries secured a $30 million contract to 3D print jet engines for the USAF, which are reportedly cheaper to build, use, and service. The 3D-printed engines will be tested for use in drones and long-range weapons. This partnership aims to accelerate technology development and cost savings.
insight: Revolutionizing military tech with 3D-printed, cost-effective jet engines.

title: Linux 7.0 enables three new AI-specific keys for keyboards, an apparent expansion beyond the Copilot key Google authors both the HID spec and the kernel patch
summary: Linux 7.0 kernel supports three new AI-specific keycodes for laptops. These keycodes are likely for dedicated AI agent keys. The addition is an expansion of AI-related keyboard features.
insight: Enabling AI-specific keys on laptops boosts AI-assisted productivity capabilities.

title: $27 platypus PCIe adapter converts half-height GPUs into full-height while adding two M.2 slots for SSDs enthusiast demos low-profile RTX 4060 with two SSDs thanks to PCIe bifurcation
summary: A PCIe adapter converts half-height GPUs to full-height and adds M.2 slots via PCIe bifurcation, expanding storage and GPU capabilities.
insight: Enabling compact builds with high-performance GPUs and SSDs simultaneously.

title: South Korea s telecom giants surprise 7 million users with unlimited, universal internet net access declared a 'basic telecommunications right,' 400 Kbps data after monthly plans run out
summary: South Korea's telecom giants offer unlimited 400 Kbps data to 7 million users. This benefit is declared as a 'basic telecommunications right.' The service will be provided after monthly plans run out.
insight: Universal internet access becomes a basic right in South Korea.

title: Valve engineer shocks Linux community with game-changing VRAM hack for 8GB GPUs breakthrough solution turbocharges gaming by prioritizing VRAM for games while background tasks take a back seat
summary: Natalie Vock has optimized VRAM usage for games in Linux, ensuring foreground games take priority over background tasks, preventing evictions and improving gaming performance.
insight: Optimized VRAM prioritization enhances gaming performance in Linux environments significantly.

title: Linux 7.0 Release Main changes, Arm, RISC-V, and MIPS architectures
summary: Linux 7.0 has been released with various small fixes and updates, including arch fixes for Arm, RISC-V, and MIPS architectures. The release follows Linux 6.19, which brought PCIe link encryption. Testing and the merge window for 7.1 have begun.
insight: Linux continues to evolve with AI-assisted corner case discoveries.

title: Homture Magic Frame smart photo display integrates generative AI and 60 GHz mmWave radar (Sponsored)
summary: The Homture Magic Frame is a smart photo display integrating generative AI and 60 GHz mmWave radar. It transforms static photos into interactive, living windows of memory, detecting users' presence and movement. The frame features a 10.1-inch Full HD touchscreen display and 64GB local storage.
insight: Revolutionary AI-powered photo display brings memories to life interactively.

title: UmbrelOS A Debian-based personal home cloud OS with a slick user interface
summary: UmbrelOS is a Debian-based home cloud OS with a user-friendly web interface. It offers over 300 apps for media storage, streaming, automation, and more. Initially launched for Bitcoin full nodes, it has evolved into a comprehensive home cloud platform.
insight: Transforms old devices into user-friendly home cloud centers easily.

title: We Tested Every Ryzen 5 and 7 X3D CPU: From 5800X3D to 9800X3D
summary: We comprehensively tested nine AMD Ryzen 5 and 7 X3D CPUs, comparing their performance in 14 games across two platforms to determine the performance improvements since the 5800X3D.
insight: X3D performance gains matter in CPU-bound and high-resolution gaming scenarios.

title: Rockstar Games hit with ransom demand after third-party data breach
summary: Rockstar Games was hit with a ransom demand after a data breach. The attackers exploited access from Anodot, a cloud analytics tool, to gain unauthorized access to Rockstar's systems. ShinyHunters claims it didn't breach Rockstar directly.
insight: Third-party tool vulnerabilities can compromise even secure cloud environments.

title: Blu-ray lives on as Verbatim and I-O Data pledge support with new drives and discs
summary: Verbatim and I-O Data partner to maintain Blu-ray supply in Japan, catering to individuals and businesses who still use optical discs for recording and archiving.
insight: Optical disc demand persists despite digital distribution growth

title: The software that landed Apollo 11 on the moon is now free online
summary: NASA has made the Apollo 11 mission's software publicly available online, free to access. The repository contains two distinct programs: Comanche055 and Luminary099. These programs played a crucial role in landing the first humans on the moon.
insight: Historical innovation now accessible to inspire future space exploration
